narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as a tough one—66% chance of not passing, based on patterns from thousands of students like you. Picture your first-year grades landing you in the bottom decile of your school—that was the biggest signal to the model, whispering that early struggles often snowball into bar exam hurdles, no matter how hard you push later. Your third-year decile sitting at 1.0 reinforced that story, painting a picture of consistent challenges in keeping up with the pack. Then there's your LSAT score of 29, which the model flagged as another strong pull toward difficulty, like a headwind that slows down even determined runners in this marathon. Your middle family income added to the caution, as it sometimes means fewer resources for the intense prep that top passers lean on. Being Asian and female didn't help in the model's eyes either, reflecting subtle patterns in who faces steeper odds. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 and full-time study plans rounded out the concerns, suggesting the academic foundation and workload might tip the scales against you. It's not that you're doomed—far from it—but together, these pieces formed a profile the model has seen falter before. The good news? You're targeting law school with clear intent, and with targeted boosts like LSAT retakes or study group strategies, you could rewrite this narrative. You've got the drive; now it's about stacking the odds in your favor. Hang in there—this is just one model's snapshot, not your full story.
